+**Structural-evidence summary.** Five structurally distinct invalidation
+shapes are visible:
+
+1. **`kernel/graph-foundation`** is a pure caller-driven router (caller
+   supplies `dependents_of`); the substrate does NOT own edge state.
+   BFS-deduplication is correct by construction and tested. No measured
+   cost gate exists; the §1.10.4 reverse-dep-radius preflight is the
+   closest current evidence and shows ~11.1 pp headroom.
+2. **`crates/cad-core::TessellationCache`** is an unbounded hash-keyed
+   memoization with explicit caller-owned hit/miss accounting and a
+   manual `clear()` boundary; tolerance is quantized so epsilon-different
+   f32 keys collide. Structural risk: unbounded growth without an LRU
+   policy. No current pressure (no measured size or hit-rate).
+3. **`crates/cad-projection::ProjectionCache`** uses
+   **head-advance ⇒ every-entity-dirty** as its explicit MVP
+   invalidation policy. This is the sharpest structural cavity in the
+   audit's required surfaces, but it is named in code as a deliberate
+   v0 simplification (`mod.rs:14-18`) and the Phase 7.3 gate-closure
+   test cleared it for 10 entities × 100 mutations structurally —
+   without a wall-time or density measurement.
+4. **`crates/gfx` frame-graph** runs a one-shot recompile per topology
+   change with deterministic ordering and per-frame Arc-deduped
+   resource-map; transient pools rotate on a tight N=2 ring. No
+   incremental compile; no per-frame compile-time wall-time gate.
+   Structural state is locked by ADR-118 D2-D6 and protected by smokes.
+5. **Script hot-reload** is the only axis with both a measured Phase 3
+   gate (0.8 % of budget) and an in-substrate snapshot mechanism — the
+   capture-snapshot-restore protocol is correct on Counter-only payload
+   per the module-doc's explicit prototype limitation
+   (`crates/script-host/src/swap.rs:7-14`).

+**Structural-evidence summary.** Five structurally distinct invalidation
+shapes are visible:
+
+1. **`kernel/graph-foundation`** is a pure caller-driven router (caller
+   supplies `dependents_of`); the substrate does NOT own edge state.
+   BFS-deduplication is correct by construction and tested. No measured
+   cost gate exists; the §1.10.4 reverse-dep-radius preflight is the
+   closest current evidence and shows ~11.1 pp headroom.
+2. **`crates/cad-core::TessellationCache`** is an unbounded hash-keyed
+   memoization with explicit caller-owned hit/miss accounting and a
+   manual `clear()` boundary; tolerance is quantized so epsilon-different
+   f32 keys collide. Structural risk: unbounded growth without an LRU
+   policy. No current pressure (no measured size or hit-rate).
+3. **`crates/cad-projection::ProjectionCache`** uses
+   **head-advance ⇒ every-entity-dirty** as its explicit MVP
+   invalidation policy. This is the sharpest structural cavity in the
+   audit's required surfaces, but it is named in code as a deliberate
+   v0 simplification (`mod.rs:15-18`) and the Phase 7.3 gate-closure
+   test cleared it for 10 entities × 100 mutations structurally —
+   without a wall-time or density measurement.
+4. **`crates/gfx` frame-graph** runs a one-shot recompile per topology
+   change with deterministic ordering and per-frame Arc-deduped
+   resource-map; transient pools rotate on a tight N=2 ring. No
+   incremental compile; no per-frame compile-time wall-time gate.
+   Structural state is locked by ADR-118 D2-D6 and protected by smokes.
+5. **Script hot-reload** is the only axis with both a measured Phase 3
+   gate (0.8 % of budget) and an in-substrate snapshot mechanism — the
+   capture-snapshot-restore protocol is correct on Counter-only payload
+   per the module-doc's explicit prototype limitation
+   (`crates/script-host/src/swap.rs:7-13`).

+**Structural-evidence summary.** Five structurally distinct invalidation
+shapes are visible:
+
+1. **`kernel/graph-foundation`** is a pure caller-driven router (caller
+   supplies `dependents_of`); the substrate does NOT own edge state.
+   BFS-deduplication is correct by construction and tested. No measured
+   cost gate exists; the Â§1.10.4 reverse-dep-radius preflight is the
+   closest current evidence and shows ~11.1 pp headroom.
+2. **`crates/cad-core::TessellationCache`** is an unbounded hash-keyed
+   memoization with explicit caller-owned hit/miss accounting and a
+   manual `clear()` boundary; tolerance is quantized so epsilon-different
+   f32 keys collide. Structural risk: unbounded growth without an LRU
+   policy. No current pressure (no measured size or hit-rate).
+3. **`crates/cad-projection::ProjectionCache`** uses
+   **head-advance â‡’ every-entity-dirty** as its explicit MVP
+   invalidation policy. This is the sharpest structural cavity in the
+   audit's required surfaces, but it is named in code as a deliberate
+   v0 simplification (`mod.rs:15-18`) and the Phase 7.3 gate-closure
+   test cleared it for 10 entities Ã— 100 mutations structurally â€”
+   without a wall-time or density measurement.
+4. **`crates/gfx` frame-graph** runs a one-shot recompile per topology
+   change with deterministic ordering and per-frame Arc-deduped
+   resource-map; transient pools rotate on a tight N=2 ring. No
+   incremental compile; no per-frame compile-time wall-time gate.
+   Structural state is locked by ADR-118 D2-D6 and protected by smokes.
+5. **Script hot-reload** is the only axis with both a measured Phase 3
+   gate (0.8 % of budget) and an in-substrate snapshot mechanism â€” the
+   capture-snapshot-restore protocol is correct on Counter-only payload
+   per the module-doc's explicit prototype limitation
